info.title: Bachs API
info.description:
The Bachs API lets you accept payments, manage subscriptions, and move money across African markets.

- **Authentication:** pass your secret key as `Authorization: Bearer sk_...`. See [Authentication](/authentication) for keys, scopes, and sandbox vs production.
- **Errors:** every error returns a flat `{ "detail", "error_code", "doc_url" }`. See [Errors](/errors).
- **Pagination:** list endpoints return `{ items, pagination }`. See [Pagination](/guides/pagination).
- **Idempotency:** retry `POST` safely with the `Idempotency-Key` header. See [Idempotency](/guides/idempotency).
- **Base URLs and rate limits:** see the [Introduction](/api-reference/overview).

tags.name: Disputes
tags.description: Respond to chargebacks and manage dispute evidence

tags.name: Conversions
tags.description: Quote, execute, and query currency conversions

tags.name: Products
tags.description: Define and manage your billing catalog

tags.name: Media
tags.description: Upload files and get an upload_id to attach to products

tags.name: Checkouts
tags.description: Create and manage API-driven checkouts without products

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Recurring billing. Subscriptions are created through checkout; manage them here.

get.description: Get all available payment methods and their supported currencies. Use this to determine which payment options to show customers.
Get all supported fiat and cryptocurrency codes. Use this to validate currency selections and display currency options to users.

Get available payment rails for a specific payment method and currency combination. Use this to determine which payment rails are available before creating a quote. The 'id' field from the response should be used as the 'payment_rail' parameter when creating quotes.

parameters.name: payment_method
parameters.description: Payment method to get rails for

parameters.name: country_code
parameters.description: Optional ISO country code (e.g., 'NG', 'GH') to filter rails by country

get.description: Get all currencies that support payouts/withdrawals, organized by fiat and cryptocurrency types.

post.description: Create a product-based checkout session

get.description: Check the status of a payment charge. Use this to poll payment status or verify completion after webhook notifications.

parameters.name: charge_id
parameters.description: Charge ID from checkout or payment

get.description: Return a paginated list of payments your organization has received, newest first. Filter with query parameters for reconciliation and monitoring. List items carry a summary of each payment; call Retrieve a payment for the full object including fees, products, and status history.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 50. Maximum is 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of records to skip before returning results.

parameters.name: status_filter
parameters.description: Optional exact status filter for charge records.

get.description: Retrieve a single payment by its charge ID, with the full object: amount, status, the customer, fees, the products paid for, refunds, and status history.
Return organization balance buckets by currency, including available, locked, and pending amounts, plus a consolidated USD total.

post.description: Create a quote for converting between settlement currencies (USD <-> NGN).

get.description:
List conversion records for your organization with offset pagination.

Common errors:
- `400 VALIDATION_ERROR`: One or more query parameters are invalid. Resolution: correct query values and retry.
- `401 UNAUTHORIZED`: API key is missing, invalid, or revoked. Resolution: use a valid API key in the `Authorization` header.

post.description: Execute a conversion between settlement currencies (USD <-> NGN) using a valid quote_id.

get.description:
Fetch a single conversion by ID.

Common errors:
- `401 UNAUTHORIZED`: API key is missing, invalid, or revoked. Resolution: use a valid API key in the `Authorization` header.
- `404 NOT_FOUND`: No conversion exists for this `conversion_id`. Resolution: verify the conversion ID and retry.
Get all currencies that support payouts/withdrawals.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Payout method to resolve currencies for.

post.description: Get a quote for a payout/withdrawal before creating it. Shows fees and exchange rates.

get.description: List all configured payout destinations (bank accounts, mobile money, crypto wallets) for your organization.

post.description: Add a new payout destination (bank account, mobile money, or crypto wallet) where you can withdraw funds.

put.description:
Update an existing payout destination.

Common errors:
- `400 VALIDATION_ERROR`: Invalid payload for destination type. Resolution: validate request fields and retry.
- `401 UNAUTHORIZED`: API key is missing, invalid, or revoked. Resolution: use a valid API key in the `Authorization` header.
- `404 NOT_FOUND`: `destination_id` was not found. Resolution: verify destination ID and retry.

post.description: Validate bank account details before creating a payout destination. Verifies account number and bank code are valid.

get.description: Get list of supported banks for bank account payouts.

parameters.name: country_code
parameters.description: ISO country code used for bank list lookup.

get.description:
Get details of your organization including payment methods, settings, and configuration.

Common errors:
- `401 UNAUTHORIZED`: Authorization is missing, invalid, or revoked. Resolution: send a valid bearer credential.
- `404 NOT_FOUND`: Organization could not be resolved from auth context. Resolution: verify credentials and organization access.

Get details of a specific organization by ID.

Common errors:
- `401 UNAUTHORIZED`: API key is missing, invalid, or revoked. Resolution: use a valid API key in the `Authorization` header.
- `404 NOT_FOUND`: No organization matches `organization_id`. Resolution: verify the ID and retry.
List all connected accounts (sub-organizations) linked to your main organization.

post.description:
Replay a previously generated webhook event by creating a new outbound delivery attempt. Use this when your endpoint missed or rejected an earlier delivery and you need Bachs to send that event again.

Common errors:
- `400 BAD_REQUEST`: No supported lookup field was provided. Resolution: provide at least one of `event_id`, `charge_id`, or `reference`.
- `401 UNAUTHORIZED`: Authorization is missing, invalid, or revoked. Resolution: send a valid bearer credential.
- `404 NOT_FOUND`: No matching webhook event could be resolved for your lookup values. Resolution: verify IDs/references and retry.

event_id.description: Webhook event ID to replay directly.

charge_id.description: Charge ID used to resolve the latest webhook event for that charge.

reference.description: Checkout reference used to resolve charge and replay latest webhook event.

schema.description: Provide at least one identifier. If `event_id` is provided, that exact event is replayed. Otherwise, the replay target is resolved by `charge_id` or `reference`.

200.description: Success - replay request accepted and a new delivery attempt was created.

attempt_no.description: Attempt sequence number for that event.

get.description: Retrieve checkout configuration for your organization context, including enabled payment methods, per-method currency toggles, and fee preference.

parameters.name: X-Connected-Account-ID
parameters.description: Connected account ID when acting on behalf of a sub-organization.

put.description: Update checkout configuration for your organization context, including enabled payment methods and fee preference.

parameters.name: X-Connected-Account-ID
parameters.description: Connected account ID when acting on behalf of a sub-organization.

schema.description: Provide one or both fields: `enabled_payment_methods` and `fee_preference`.

get.description: List payout withdrawals for your organization.
Get a payout withdrawal by ID.

post.description: Create a payout withdrawal request and start payout processing.
Create a refund for a completed payment. Only one refund can be created per charge.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of refunds for your organization, ordered from most recent to oldest.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of refunds to return. Min 1, max 100. Default 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of refunds to skip before returning results. Default 0.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by refund status. Accepted values: PROCESSING, SUCCESS, FAILED.

get.description: Retrieve a single refund by its ID.

parameters.name: refund_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the refund.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of disputes for your organization. Results are returned with the most recently created disputes first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of disputes to return. Minimum 1 and maximum 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of disputes to skip before returning results.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Return disputes created at or after this timestamp (ISO 8601).

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: Return disputes created at or before this timestamp (ISO 8601).

get.description: Retrieve full details for a single dispute, including the current evidence draft and latest submission metadata.

evidence.product_description: Annual SaaS subscription, plan ID PLAN-PRO-001
evidence.notes: Customer confirmed delivery via email on March 5.

post.description: Upload a supporting document for a dispute and receive a document identifier for evidence submission.

file.description: Document file to upload (PDF, JPEG, PNG, or GIF up to 10 MB).

patch.description: Save or update dispute evidence fields before final submission. Evidence can be updated iteratively while the dispute remains editable.

example.product_description: Annual SaaS subscription, plan ID PLAN-PRO-001
example.notes: Customer confirmed delivery via email on March 5.

post.description: Submit saved dispute evidence for network review. This action is irreversible and locks further evidence edits.

requestBody.description: This endpoint does not require a request body. Submit all dispute evidence beforehand via the evidence update endpoint.

schema.description: No request fields are accepted for this operation.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of your customers, most recent first. Pass `search` to filter by email or name. See [Pagination](/guides/pagination) for how to page through results. Requires the `customers:read` scope.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by customer email or name.

post.description: Creates a customer. A customer groups a buyer's payments, subscriptions, and saved payment methods under one record. Only `email` is required. Requires the `customers:write` scope.

get.description: Retrieves a single customer by ID. Requires the `customers:read` scope.

patch.description: Updates a customer. Only the fields you send are changed. Requires the `customers:write` scope.

post.description: Creates a product with its pricing. Every product has a `price`; add a `billing_cycle` to make it recurring, or omit it for a one-time product. Sell products through checkout sessions and subscriptions. Requires the `products:write` scope.

example.name: Pro Plan
example.description: Monthly access to all Pro features.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of your products, most recent first. Archived products are excluded unless you pass `include_archived=true`. See [Pagination](/guides/pagination) for how to page through results. Requires the `products:read` scope.

parameters.name: include_archived
parameters.description: Include archived products in the results. Defaults to `false`.

get.description: Retrieves a single product by its ID. Requires the `products:read` scope.

patch.description: Updates a product. You can change its name, description, metadata, media, price, and (if not yet set) its `billing_cycle` and `trial_period`. A `billing_cycle` is immutable once set, so a recurring product's interval cannot be changed. Create a new product for a different cadence. Requires the `products:write` scope.

post.description: Archives a product so it can no longer be used in new checkouts or subscriptions. Existing subscriptions keep billing. This is idempotent: archiving an already-archived product succeeds. Reverse it with [Unarchive Product](/api-reference/products/unarchive-product). Requires the `products:write` scope.
Restores an archived product to active status so it can be used again. This is idempotent. Requires the `products:write` scope.
Bundle two or more products into a group for multi-plan checkout. Requires `PRODUCTS_WRITE` scope.

get.description: Return a paginated list of product groups. Requires `PRODUCTS_READ` scope.
Fetch a product group and its member products. Requires `PRODUCTS_READ` scope.

patch.description: Rename the group and/or replace its product membership. Requires `PRODUCTS_WRITE` scope.

delete.description: Permanently delete a product group. Member products are not affected. Requires `PRODUCTS_WRITE` scope.

post.description:
Upload a file and receive an `upload_id`. Pass this ID in the `media` array when creating or updating a product.

Files must be sent as `multipart/form-data`. Maximum size is **20 MB**.

file.description: The file to upload. Maximum 20 MB.

scope.description: Logical grouping for the upload. Use `product-media` for product images. Defaults to `general`.

get.description: Retrieve metadata for a previously created upload by its ID.

delete.description: Delete an upload that has not yet been linked to any resource. Returns a `409` if the upload is already attached to a product.

get.description:
Retrieve the details of a checkout by its ID. Works for all checkout types (`API`, `CHECKOUT_SESSION`, `PAYMENT_LINK`).

The `charge` field is `null` while the checkout is `OPEN` and populated once the customer submits a payment.

Requires `payments:read` scope.

parameters.name: checkout_id
parameters.description: The checkout ID returned when the checkout was created.

get.description:
Retrieve the details of a checkout session by its ID, including resolved product line items and charge information.

The `charge` field is `null` while the session is `OPEN` and populated once the customer submits a payment.

Requires `payments:read` scope.

parameters.name: checkout_id
parameters.description: The checkout ID returned when the session was created.

get.description: List subscriptions for your organization, newest first. Filter by customer or status.
Retrieve a single subscription with its product, price, items, and billing dates.

patch.description: Change a subscription. Send exactly one intent: change the plan, move a trial, or change the payment method. Returns the full updated subscription.

delete.description: Cancel a subscription immediately, or at the end of the current period with cancel_at_period_end. Returns the updated subscription.

get.description: Retrieve the refund associated with a specific payment by `payment_id`.

parameters.name: payment_id
parameters.description: The ID of the payment whose refund you want to retrieve.

post.description: Register a URL to receive webhook events, and choose which events to subscribe to. The signing secret is returned once in the response. Requires the `webhooks:write` scope.

201.description: The created endpoint, including its signing secret.

get.description: List all webhook endpoints for your organization. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.
Retrieve a single webhook endpoint by ID. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.

patch.description: Update an endpoint's name, URL, or subscribed events. Only the fields you send are changed. Requires the `webhooks:write` scope.

delete.description: Delete a webhook endpoint. It stops receiving events immediately. Requires the `webhooks:write` scope.

get.description: Retrieve the current signing secret for an endpoint. Use it to verify the `X-Bachs-Signature` header on deliveries. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.

200.description: The endpoint and its signing secret.

post.description: Generate a new signing secret for an endpoint. The old secret stops working immediately, so update your verification before rotating. Requires the `webhooks:write` scope.

200.description: The endpoint with its secret rotated.

get.description: Retrieve delivery success and failure counts for an endpoint over a time range. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.
List the events delivered (or attempted) to a specific endpoint. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.
Retrieve one event's full payload and delivery attempts for a specific endpoint. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.

200.description: The event with its payload and attempts.

post.summary: Resend an event to an endpoint
post.description: Re-deliver a past event to a specific endpoint. Requires the `webhooks:write` scope.

get.description: List all webhook events for your organization, across every endpoint. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.
Retrieve a single event's full payload and delivery attempts. Requires the `webhooks:read` scope.

200.description: The event with its payload and attempts.

ApiKeyAuth.description: Bearer token authentication. Pass your API key as `Authorization: Bearer sk_...`. See [Authentication](/authentication) for keys, scopes, and sandbox vs production.

currency.description: Base currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'NGN'). Must be a supported fiat currency.

amount.description: Base amount as decimal string

currency_options.description: Currency-specific pricing overrides. Keys are fiat currency codes, values are decimal amount strings.

product_id.description: Product ID to include in checkout.

quantity.description: Number of units for the product item.

amount.description: Optional custom amount override for CUSTOM-priced products.

name.description: Customer full name.

oneOf.description: Checkout with a fixed cart of product items.
Checkout from a product collection (customer selects a product).

billing_currency.description: Optional checkout billing currency. If omitted, defaults to product pricing currency.

allowed_payment_method_types.description: Optional list of allowed payment methods.

cancel_url.description: Where to send the customer if they cancel or abandon the checkout. Returned on the checkout so the hosted page can route back to it.

return_url.description: Deprecated alias for `success_url`, kept for backward compatibility. If both are set, `success_url` wins.

success_url.description: Where to redirect the customer after a successful payment. Bachs appends `?checkout_id=<id>`. This is the primary success-redirect field.

customer.description: Customer details for the checkout session.

product_cart.description: List of product items to include in this checkout session. Mutually exclusive with `product_collection_id`.

product_collection_id.description: ID of a product collection to use for this checkout session. The customer selects a product from the collection. Mutually exclusive with `product_cart`.

reference.description: Optional client reference (unique per organization). If not provided, one will be auto-generated. Used for idempotency and tracking.

expires_in_minutes.description: Minutes until the checkout session expires. Defaults to 60. After expiry the checkout URL is invalid.

charge_id.description: The ID of the payment to refund.

reference.description: Your unique identifier for this refund. Must be unique per organization and environment.

refund_address.description: Destination wallet address for crypto refunds. Required when the charge currency is a cryptocurrency.

amount.description: Optional partial refund amount in the charge settlement currency. Omit to refund the full remaining refundable balance.

fee_bearer.description: Who bears the refund processing fee. `org` (default) charges the fee to your balance; `customer` deducts it from the amount returned to the customer.

idempotency_key.description: A key you supply to make this request idempotent. If you send the same idempotency_key twice for the same charge, the second request returns the existing refund.

simulated_outcome.description: Test mode only. Force a specific refund outcome. Omit to use the default sandbox outcome.

charge_id.description: The payment this refund is associated with.

reference.description: The reference you supplied on creation.

status.description: Current refund status. `processing` = awaiting provider (1-5 business days); `success` = funds returned to customer; `failed` = refund rejected.

requested_amount.description: The refund amount you requested, in the charge's settlement currency.

refunded_amount.description: The amount actually returned to the customer. Null until the refund completes or partially settles.

refund_fee_amount.description: Fee charged for this refund, in the charge's settlement currency. "0" if no fee applies.

fee_bearer.description: Who bears the refund processing fee. `org` means the merchant absorbs the fee; `customer` means it is deducted from the refunded amount.

reason.description: The reason you provided, or null if none was given.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the refund was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last status update.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the refund reached a terminal status (SUCCESS or FAILED). Null while still processing.

total.description: Total number of refunds matching the query, across all pages.

items.description: Refund objects for the current page.

CreateCheckoutSessionResponse.description: Response containing checkout session details and hosted checkout URL.

checkout_id.description: Unique identifier for the underlying checkout.

checkout_url.description: Hosted checkout URL where your customer can complete payment.

status.description: Current checkout status. New sessions start in `OPEN`. `OPEN`: Awaiting customer payment. New sessions start here. `COMPLETED`: Payment succeeded. This is a terminal state. `EXPIRED`: The session window elapsed before payment. This is a terminal state. `CANCELLED`: Canceled before completion. This is a terminal state.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the checkout will expire. After this time, customers cannot complete payment through this checkout.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the checkout was created.

payment_rail.description: Selected payment rail identifier. Fetch available rails by calling GET /v1/payments/rails?payment_method={method}&currency={currency}. Use the 'id' field from the response. Required when multiple rails are available for the payment method and currency combination.

customer_email.description: Customer email address (optional; defaults to system identity for provider-specific quotes)

customer_name.description: Customer name (optional; defaults to a system identity when omitted)

QuoteResponse.description: External response for quote (all payment methods) - excludes provider and gateway details

payment_method.description: Payment method used to generate quote

payment_rail.description: Payment rail used to generate quote

amount_local_base.description: Amount in local currency (what customer pays)

currency.description: Local currency code (e.g., 'NGN', 'TZS', 'USD')

exchange_rate.description: Exchange rate (Decimal as string, if applicable)

ChargeStatusResponse.description: Complete payment details including current status, payment information, and status history

charge_id.description: Unique identifier for this payment/payment. Use this ID to track the payment status.

organization_id.description: Organization ID that created this payment.

customer_id.description: Customer identifier associated with this payment.

amount.description: Amount the customer paid, in the payment currency. This is a decimal string for precision.

currency.description: Currency code that the customer paid in (e.g., 'NGN', 'USD', 'GHS').

settlement_currency.description: Currency code you will receive settlement in. This may differ from the payment currency.

settlement_amount.description: Amount you will receive after fees are deducted, in settlement_currency. This is a decimal string for precision.

status.description: Current status of the payment. PENDING, AWAITING_PAYMENT, and PROCESSING are non-final states. COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED, EXPIRED, and REFUNDED are final states.

metadata.description: Custom metadata you provided when creating the checkout. This can include order IDs, product SKUs, or any other relevant information.

status_history.description: Chronological history of all status changes for this payment. Useful for tracking the payment lifecycle.

status.description: Status at this point in time

occurred_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this status change occurred

provider_reference.description: Transaction reference from the payment provider, if available

reason.description: Optional reason or description for this status change

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the payment was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the payment was last updated.

icon.description: Icon or token representing the method.

enabled_by_default.description: Whether enabled by default for organizations.

payment_methods.description: List of payment methods available to the authenticated organization in the current environment.

SupportedCurrenciesResponse.description: Response containing all supported currencies organized by type (fiat and cryptocurrency)

fiat.description: List of supported fiat (traditional) currencies. These are government-issued currencies like USD, NGN, GHS.

crypto.description: List of supported cryptocurrency codes. These may include network identifiers (e.g., 'USDT_TRC20' for Tron network, 'USDT_ERC20' for Ethereum network).

AccountBalanceResponse.description: Organization balance snapshot across currencies, including spendable and in-flight amounts.

account_id.description: Organization ID the returned balances belong to.

balances.description: One balance object per currency bucket.

currency.description: Currency code for this bucket (ISO 4217 or configured crypto code).

available_balance.description: Amount currently available for new operations in this currency.

pending_balance.description: In-flight amount not yet available for spending.

total_balance_usd.description: Aggregate of available and pending balances converted to USD.

pending_settlements_by_day.description: Upcoming settlements grouped by day. Empty when no settlements are pending. Each entry describes settlements expected to become available on a given date.

PayoutSupportedCurrenciesResponse.description: Currencies currently supported for payout flows, grouped by type.

amount.description: Amount in `from_currency` to quote.

to_currency.description: Currency to send to payout destination.

amount.description: Amount to withdraw in `from_currency`.

email.description: Contact email for payout processing context.

account_number.description: Bank account number for bank-transfer payouts.

network.description: Network identifier for crypto payouts when required.

CreateWithdrawalResponse.description: Response returned after creating a withdrawal request.

status.description: Current withdrawal status.

currency.description: Currency code

PayoutDestinationResponse.description: Response containing payout destination details (bank account, mobile money, or crypto wallet)

id.description: Unique identifier for this payout destination. Use this ID when creating withdrawals.

organization_id.description: Organization ID that owns this destination.

env.description: Environment this destination belongs to. 'test' for test mode, 'live' for production.

destination_type.description: Type of payout destination. Determines which fields are populated.

currency.description: Currency code this destination accepts (e.g., 'NGN', 'USD', 'USDT_TRC20'). Withdrawals to this destination must use this currency.

label.description: User-friendly name for this destination. Helps you identify destinations in your system.

account_number.description: Bank account number. Only populated for bank_account destinations.

account_name.description: Account holder name as registered with the bank. Only populated for bank_account destinations.

bank_code.description: Bank code or routing number. Only populated for bank_account destinations.

bank_name.description: Full name of the bank. Only populated for bank_account destinations.

phone_number.description: Phone number associated with the mobile money account. Only populated for mobile_money destinations.

mobile_provider.description: Mobile money provider (e.g., 'MTN', 'Vodafone'). Only populated for mobile_money destinations.

wallet_address.description: Cryptocurrency wallet address. Only populated for crypto_wallet destinations.

network.description: Blockchain network (e.g., 'TRC20', 'ERC20'). Only populated for crypto_wallet destinations. May be included in currency code (e.g., 'USDT_TRC20').

is_active.description: Whether this destination is active and can receive payouts. Inactive destinations cannot be used for withdrawals.

metadata.description: Additional custom metadata associated with this destination.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this destination was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this destination was last updated.

PayoutDestinationListResponse.description: Response containing list of all configured payout destinations

total.description: Total number of payout destinations for your organization.

BankAccountResolveResponse.description: Response containing bank account validation and resolution details

status.description: Whether the bank account was successfully resolved. true if account details are valid, false if invalid.

message.description: Human-readable message describing the resolution result.

data.description: Resolved bank account details. Only present when status is true. Contains account_name, bank_name, and other validated information.

error.description: Error message if account resolution failed. Only present when status is false.

status.description: Whether the request succeeded.

name.description: Bank display name.

slug.description: Provider-specific stable slug for the bank.

code.description: Bank code used for payout account resolution.

country.description: Country code where the bank operates.

PayoutResponse.description: Payout/withdrawal response. Core fields are always present; extended fields may be null when not populated by the current endpoint flow.

amount.description: Withdrawal amount.

currency.description: Withdrawal currency code.

status.description: Current withdrawal status.

x-enum-descriptions.pending_submission: Withdrawal created and queued for submission to the processor.
x-enum-descriptions.pending_collection: Funds are being collected from the source account.
x-enum-descriptions.processing: Withdrawal is being processed by the payment network.
x-enum-descriptions.manual_review: Withdrawal flagged for manual review before proceeding.
x-enum-descriptions.successful: Withdrawal completed and funds delivered to the destination.
x-enum-descriptions.failed: Withdrawal failed; no funds were moved.
x-enum-descriptions.expired: Withdrawal expired before processing could complete.
x-enum-descriptions.reconciled: Withdrawal settled and reconciled in the ledger.

parent_withdrawal_id.description: Parent withdrawal ID for batched payouts.

created_at.description: Creation timestamp.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp.

PayoutListResponse.description: Response containing paginated list of payouts/withdrawals

total.description: Total number of payouts matching the query criteria, across all pages.

items.description: Array of payout objects for the current page

id.description: Rail identifier. Use this value as the 'payment_rail' parameter when creating quotes.

name.description: Human-readable rail name

active.description: Whether the rail is currently active and available for use

PaymentRailsResponse.description: Response containing available rails for a method and currency.

currency.description: Currency requested.

country_code.description: Resolved country code for this rail lookup.

rails.description: Available rails for this method and currency.

from_currency.description: Currency to convert from (must be a settlement currency: USD or NGN)

to_currency.description: Currency to convert to (must be a settlement currency: USD or NGN)

amount.description: Amount to convert as a decimal string

ConversionQuoteResponse.description: Response containing conversion quote with exchange rate and amounts

quote_id.description: Unique identifier for this conversion quote. Use this ID when executing the conversion.

from_amount.description: Amount in from_currency as a decimal string

to_amount.description: Amount in to_currency as a decimal string

exchange_rate.description: Exchange rate applied (1 from_currency = X to_currency)

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this quote expires

from_currency.description: Currency to convert from (must match the quote)

to_currency.description: Currency to convert to (must match the quote)

amount.description: Amount to convert as a decimal string (must match the quote)

quote_id.description: Quote ID from the create conversion quote endpoint

conversion_id.description: Unique identifier for this conversion transaction

status.description: Status of the conversion. `pending` = in progress (typically seconds to minutes); `completed` = conversion successful and rate is final; `failed` = conversion could not be completed.

from_amount.description: Amount converted from as a decimal string

to_amount.description: Amount converted to as a decimal string

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the conversion was executed

quote_id.description: Quote identifier used for this conversion, if conversion was executed from a quote.

metadata.description: Optional metadata associated with the conversion.

total.description: Total number of conversions that match the filter criteria.

limit.description: Maximum number of conversion records returned in this page.

offset.description: Number of conversion records skipped before this page.

OrganizationResponse.description: Response containing organization details, settings, and configuration

name.description: Organization name as registered.

owner_user_id.description: User ID of the organization owner.

parent_organization_id.description: Parent organization ID if this is a connected account. Null for main organizations.

onboarding_status.description: Current onboarding status. Possible values: PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED.

payments_status.description: Payment processing status. Must be ENABLED to process payments. Possible values: DISABLED, ENABLED.

country.description: ISO country code where the organization is based (e.g., 'NG', 'GH', 'US').

fee_handling.description: Fee handling mode. 'org_pays_fee' means your organization pays fees (you receive amount minus fees). 'customer_pays_fee' means customer pays fees (customer pays amount plus fees, you receive full amount).

enabled_payment_methods.description: Configuration of which payment methods are enabled for your organization. Each method shows if it's enabled and which currencies it supports.

enabled.description: Whether this payment method is enabled for your organization

currencies.description: List of currency codes this payment method supports

is_active.description: Whether the organization is active. Inactive organizations cannot process payments.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the organization was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the organization was last updated.

Error.description: Standard error response format used across all API endpoints

detail.description: Human-readable error message explaining what went wrong.

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code. Use this to handle errors programmatically. Common codes: VALIDATION_ERROR, UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, TOO_MANY_REQUESTS, INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, BAD_GATEWAY, SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE.

errors.description: Optional array of field-level validation errors. Only present for validation errors (400).

message.example: Amount must be a positive decimal string

SupportedPayoutCurrenciesByMethodRespons.description: Payout currencies supported for a specific method.

method.description: Requested payout method, normalized to uppercase.

currencies.description: Currencies available for this payout method.

success.description: Boolean result indicating whether the destination was successfully deactivated.

DisputeSummary.description: Summary representation of a dispute record.

amount.description: Disputed amount as a decimal string.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount.

status.description: Current dispute status.

is_response_editable.description: Whether evidence can still be updated and submitted.

reason.description: Dispute reason code reported by the payment network.

response_deadline_at.description: Evidence submission deadline in ISO 8601 format.

created_at.description: Dispute creation timestamp.

updated_at.description: Most recent update timestamp.

total.description: Total number of disputes matching the query.

items.description: Dispute records for the requested page.

DisputeEvidence.description: Evidence fields attached to a dispute response.

cancellation_policy_disclosure.description: Cancellation policy shown to the customer.

notes.description: Additional context supporting the dispute response.

product_description.description: Description of delivered goods or services.

refund_policy_disclosure.description: Refund policy shown to the customer.

refund_refusal_explanation.description: Reason a refund was not granted.

DisputeSubmission.description: Metadata for a dispute evidence submission attempt.

submission_id.description: Unique identifier for this submission attempt.

status.description: Submission delivery status.

failed_at.description: Timestamp when submission failed, if applicable.

DisputeResponse.description: Detailed dispute payload including evidence and latest submission metadata.

amount.description: Disputed amount as a decimal string.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount.

status.description: Current dispute status.

is_response_editable.description: Whether evidence is still editable for this dispute.

reason.description: Dispute reason code reported by the payment network.

response_deadline_at.description: Evidence submission deadline in ISO 8601 format.

latest_submission.description: Most recent submission attempt for this dispute.

created_at.description: Dispute creation timestamp.

updated_at.description: Most recent update timestamp.

mime_type.description: Detected MIME type of the uploaded file.

DisputeEvidenceUpdateRequest.description: Fields used to create or update dispute evidence. Include only fields that should change.

cancellation_policy_disclosure.description: Cancellation policy shown to the customer.

notes.description: Additional context supporting the dispute response.

product_description.description: Description of delivered goods or services.

refund_policy_disclosure.description: Refund policy shown to the customer.

refund_refusal_explanation.description: Reason a refund was not granted.

DisputeEvidenceUpdateResponse.description: Response after saving dispute evidence fields.

status.description: Current dispute status after update.

is_response_editable.description: Whether the dispute is still editable after update.

evidence_updated_at.description: Timestamp when evidence was last updated.

DisputeSubmitResponse.description: Response returned after submitting dispute evidence for review.

status.description: Updated dispute status after submission.

is_response_editable.description: Whether the dispute remains editable after submission.

submission.description: Metadata for the submission attempt created by this request.

submission_id.description: Unique identifier for this submission attempt.

name.description: Display name of the product. Shown to customers at checkout.

description.description: Optional description of the product. Shown to customers at checkout.
description.example: Monthly access to all Pro features.

price.description: Primary price for the product. Provide an `amount` as a decimal string and a `currency`.

metadata.description: Up to 20 key/value pairs for your own reference.

billing_cycle.description: How often the product bills. Provide a cadence to make the product recurring, or omit it for a one-time product.

trial_period.description: A free trial before the first charge, given as a duration like `{ "interval": "day", "frequency": 14 }` for 14 days. The customer isn't charged until the trial ends. Only valid on a recurring product. Currently in beta.

name.description: New display name for the product.

media.description: Ordered list of upload IDs. Replaces existing media.

price.description: Price fields to update. Omit to leave price unchanged.

billing_cycle.description: How often the product bills. Provide a cadence to make the product recurring, or omit it for a one-time product.

trial_period.description: A free trial before the first charge, given as a duration like `{ "interval": "day", "frequency": 14 }` for 14 days. The customer isn't charged until the trial ends. Only valid on a recurring product. Currently in beta.

id.description: Unique identifier for the product, prefixed with `prod_`.

organization_id.description: The organization that owns the product.

name.description: Display name of the product.

description.description: Optional description of the product. `null` when not set.
description.example: Monthly access to all Pro features.

price.description: Primary price for the product, in the product's default currency.

status.description: Status of the product. `active`: Live and available for use in checkouts and subscriptions. `archived`: Retired. Kept for reference but not available for new purchases.

metadata.description: Your own key-value data attached to the product, returned unchanged.

media.description: Media items (images) attached to the product. Empty when none are set.

actor_id.description: Identifier of the user or key that created the product.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the product was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the product was last updated.

archived_at.description: When set, the product is archived and cannot be used in new checkouts. `null` while the product is active.

billing_cycle.description: How often the product bills. Provide a cadence to make the product recurring, or omit it for a one-time product.

trial_period.description: A free trial before the first charge, given as a duration like `{ "interval": "day", "frequency": 14 }` for 14 days. The customer isn't charged until the trial ends. Only valid on a recurring product. Currently in beta.

prices.description: All prices configured on the product, one per currency. Each has `currency`, `amount`, optional `minimum_amount` and `maximum_amount`, and `is_default`.

total_payments.description: Running count of completed payments for this product. Starts at `0` and increments as customers pay.

total_amount.description: Running total collected for this product, as a decimal string in the product currency. Starts at `"0.00"`.

pagination.description: Pagination cursors and counts. See the Pagination guide.

items.description: The products on this page. Each item is a product object.

linked_resource_type.description: Resource type this upload is attached to, e.g. `product`.

linked_resource_id.description: ID of the resource this upload is attached to.

customer_id.description: Unique identifier for the customer, prefixed with `cust_`.

name.description: The customer's full name. `null` when not set.

metadata.description: Your own key-value data attached to the customer.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the customer was created.

items.description: The customers on this page. Each item is a customer object.

pagination.description: Pagination cursors and counts. See the Pagination guide.

customer_id.description: Unique identifier for the customer, prefixed with `cust_`.

name.description: The customer's full name. `null` when not set.

phone_number.description: The customer's phone number in E.164 format, e.g. `+2348012345678`.

metadata.description: Your own key-value data attached to the customer.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the customer was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the customer was last updated.

email.description: The customer's email address. Used to identify the customer and send receipts.

name.description: The customer's full name. Derived from `first_name` and `last_name` when those are provided instead.

phone_number.description: The customer's phone number in E.164 format, e.g. `+2348012345678`.

metadata.description: Your own key-value data attached to the customer, returned unchanged.

email.description: The customer's email address. Used to identify the customer and send receipts.

name.description: The customer's full name. Derived from `first_name` and `last_name` when those are provided instead.

phone_number.description: The customer's phone number in E.164 format, e.g. `+2348012345678`.

metadata.description: Your own key-value data attached to the customer, returned unchanged.

currency.description: A supported additional currency (`GHS`, `KES`, `UGX`, `TZS`, `RWF`, `XAF`, `XOF`, `ZMW`). Cannot be the primary currency.

amount.description: Price as a decimal string, e.g. `"29.00"`. Required when `price_type` is `fixed`. Omit for `free` and `custom`.

preset_amount.description: Suggested amount prefilled at checkout for a custom price in this currency, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`.

minimum_amount.description: Least the customer can pay, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`. Set `"0.00"` to allow free (pay what you want).

maximum_amount.description: Most the customer can pay, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`.

currency.description: The product's primary currency. Must be `USD` or `NGN`.

price_type.description: How the product is priced. `fixed`: a set amount, given in `amount`. `free`: no charge. `custom`: the customer pays what they want, bounded by `minimum_amount` and `maximum_amount` with an optional `preset_amount` suggestion.

amount.description: Price as a decimal string, e.g. `"29.00"`. Required when `price_type` is `fixed`. Omit for `free` and `custom`.

preset_amount.description: Suggested amount prefilled at checkout for a custom price, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`.

minimum_amount.description: Least the customer can pay, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`. Set `"0.00"` to allow free (pay what you want).

maximum_amount.description: Most the customer can pay, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`.

currency_options.description: Prices in other currencies. Each entry sets a price for one additional currency, and cannot repeat the primary currency.

amount.description: New price as a decimal in the major unit (e.g. `39.00`). Only valid for fixed-price products.

currency_options.description: Full replacement of multi-currency prices. Omit to leave unchanged.

price_type.description: How the product is priced. `fixed`: a set amount, given in `amount`. `free`: no charge. `custom`: the customer pays what they want, bounded by `minimum_amount` and `maximum_amount` with an optional `preset_amount` suggestion.

amount.description: Price in the primary currency as a decimal string.

preset_amount.description: Suggested amount prefilled at checkout for a custom price, as a decimal string. Only used when `price_type` is `custom`.

minimum_amount.description: Minimum the customer must pay. Present only when `price_type` is `custom`.

maximum_amount.description: Maximum the customer may pay. Present only when `price_type` is `custom`.

ResolvedProductItem.description: A resolved product line item within a checkout session.

currency.description: Currency code for this line item.

price_type.description: How the product is priced. `fixed`: a set amount, given in `amount`. `free`: no charge. `custom`: the customer pays what they want, bounded by `minimum_amount` and `maximum_amount` with an optional `preset_amount` suggestion.

minimum_amount.description: Minimum allowed amount when `price_type` is true.

maximum_amount.description: Maximum allowed amount when `price_type` is true.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the checkout. `OPEN`: Awaiting customer payment. New checkouts start here. `COMPLETED`: Payment succeeded. This is a terminal state. `EXPIRED`: The checkout window elapsed before payment. This is a terminal state. `CANCELLED`: Canceled before completion. This is a terminal state.

source_type.description: How the checkout was created. `API`: Created directly through the API. `CHECKOUT_SESSION`: Created from a checkout session. `PAYMENT_LINK`: Created from a shareable payment link.

amount.description: Requested amount in `currency`.

currency.description: Base currency code.

charge.description: The charge created when the customer submitted payment. `null` while the checkout is still `OPEN`.

customer_id.description: Linked customer ID, if the checkout was created with an existing customer.

success_url.description: URL the customer is redirected to after successful payment.

cancel_url.description: URL the customer is redirected to if they cancel.

metadata.description: Public metadata you attached at checkout creation.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 creation timestamp.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the checkout was completed.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 last-updated timestamp.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the checkout session. `OPEN`: Awaiting customer payment. New sessions start here. `COMPLETED`: Payment succeeded. This is a terminal state. `EXPIRED`: The session window elapsed before payment. This is a terminal state. `CANCELLED`: Canceled before completion. This is a terminal state.

recurring.description: Present only for a subscription checkout; `null` for a one-time checkout.

payment_status.description: Payment lifecycle for the checkout. `requires_payment_method`, `requires_confirmation`, `requires_action`, `processing`, `succeeded`, `failed`, or `canceled`.

source_type.description: What created the checkout, e.g. `CHECKOUT_SESSION` or `API`.

amount.description: Total amount in `currency`.

currency.description: Base currency code.

charge.description: The payment created by this checkout, once payment has been attempted. `null` before then.

payment_method.description: The payment method selected for the checkout, if any.

success_url.description: URL the customer is redirected to after successful payment.

cancel_url.description: URL the customer is redirected to if they cancel.

products.description: Resolved product line items. Populated for `CART` sessions; may be `null` for `SELECTION` sessions before the customer picks a product.

billing_currency.description: Currency the customer selected for billing.

session_mode.description: How products are presented. `CART` sums a fixed set of items; `SELECTION` lets the customer pick one from a group.

metadata.description: Public metadata you attached at session creation.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 creation timestamp.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the session was completed.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 last-updated timestamp.

PaymentProductItem.description: A product line item purchased in this payment.

currency.description: Currency code for this line item.

interval.description: Unit of time for each billing cycle. `day`: billed daily. `week`: billed weekly. `month`: billed monthly. `year`: billed yearly.

frequency.description: Number of intervals per cycle. For example, `interval` `month` with `frequency` `3` bills every three months.

name.description: The product's name, shown to customers at checkout.

description.description: The product's description. `null` if none was set.
description.example: Everything in Basic, plus priority support.

status.description: Whether the product is active or archived.

created_at.description: When the product was created, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the product was last updated, in UTC.

product_id.description: The product this price belongs to.

price_type.description: How this line item is priced. `fixed`: a set price per cycle, the same for every customer. `free`: no charge. `custom`: the customer chose the amount at checkout, within the product's bounds.

currency.description: The currency of this price, as an ISO 4217 code.

unit_amount.description: Decimal string at the currency's precision

seat_tiers.description: Reserved for seat-based pricing. `null` for the standard pricing available today.

is_archived.description: Whether the price has been archived. Archived prices keep billing existing subscribers but are not offered for new checkouts.

created_at.description: When the price was created, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the price was last updated, in UTC.

id.description: Unique identifier for the line item.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the item. Follows the parent subscription's status.

quantity.description: The billed quantity for this item.

recurring.description: Whether this item recurs each billing cycle. Always `true` for subscription items.

price_type.description: How this line item is priced. `fixed`: a set price per cycle, the same for every customer. `free`: no charge. `custom`: the customer chose the amount at checkout, within the product's bounds.

unit_amount.description: Price for one unit of this item, as a decimal string in the item's currency.

currency.description: The currency this item is billed in, as an ISO 4217 code.

previously_billed_at.description: When this item was last billed, in UTC. `null` if it has not been billed yet.

next_billed_at.description: When this item will next be billed, in UTC.

created_at.description: When the item was created, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the item was last updated, in UTC.

payment_method_id.description: The saved payment method billed on each renewal. `null` until a payment method is attached.

status.description: Status of the subscription. Set automatically by Bachs as payments succeed or fail. `trialing`: In a free trial. No payment has been collected yet. `trial_end` marks when billing begins. `active`: Active and paid. Bachs is billing this subscription automatically each cycle. `past_due`: A cycle payment failed. Bachs is retrying the payment while access continues. `unpaid`: Payment retries have been exhausted. Access should be restricted. `canceled`: Canceled and will not renew. This is a terminal state. `paused`: Temporarily suspended. Billing is halted until the subscription resumes.

collection_method.description: How renewals are collected. `charge_automatically` bills the saved card each cycle.

currency.description: The currency the subscription is billed in, as an ISO 4217 code. Subscriptions are USD only today.

amount.description: Recurring amount as a decimal string

quantity.description: Total billable quantity across the subscription's line items.

current_period_start.description: Start of the period currently being billed for, in UTC.

current_period_end.description: End of the period currently being billed for, in UTC. The next charge lands at this time unless the subscription is canceled first.

previously_billed_at.description: Start of the period that was last billed

trial_end.description: When the free trial ends and billing begins, in UTC. `null` if the subscription is not trialing.

cancel_at_period_end.description: When `true`, the subscription stays active until `current_period_end` and is not renewed. When `false`, it renews normally.

canceled_at.description: When the subscription was canceled, in UTC. `null` if it has not been canceled.

created_at.description: When the subscription was created, in UTC.

items.description: The line items that make up the subscription. Each item ties a product and its price to a billed quantity.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, or `null` on the last page.

prev_cursor.description: Cursor for the previous page, or `null` on the first page.

has_more.description: Whether more results exist after this page.

limit.description: The page size that was applied.

returned.description: The number of items returned on this page.

total.description: Total number of subscriptions matching the query.

UpdateSubscriptionRequest.description: A single change to a subscription. Send exactly one intent per request: change the plan (product_id), move a trial (trial_end), or change the payment method (payment_method_id). Combining intents returns 400.

product_id.description: Move the subscription to this product (plan). The price is resolved from the product for the subscription's currency.

trial_end.description: Future = add/extend the trial; past-or-now = end it and bill now.

payment_method_id.description: Point the subscription at a different saved card. If past_due/unpaid, retries immediately. Stands alone.

proration_behavior.description: How a plan change is settled. Defaults to invoice_now. See the Proration guide.

cancel_at_period_end.description: true = cancel at current_period_end; false = cancel immediately.

reason.description: An optional free-text note recording why the subscription was canceled. Max 255 characters.

customer_id.description: Unique identifier for the customer, prefixed with `cust_`.

name.description: The customer's full name. `null` when not set.

phone_number.description: The customer's phone number in E.164 format, e.g. `+2348012345678`.

metadata.description: Your own key-value data attached to the customer.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the customer was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the customer was last updated.

PaymentResponse.description: Detailed payment response for API integrations.

billing_reason.description: Why this payment exists. `purchase`: a one-time purchase. `subscription_create`: the first cycle of a new subscription. `subscription_cycle`: a subscription renewal. `subscription_update`: an off-cycle charge from a mid-cycle plan change (proration).

status.description: payment status.

x-enum-descriptions.accepted: Payment accepted by the processor, pending final confirmation.
x-enum-descriptions.expired: Charge expired before payment was received.
x-enum-descriptions.underpaid: Payment received was less than the requested amount.
x-enum-descriptions.overpaid: Payment received exceeded the requested amount.

is_refundable.description: Whether this payment can currently be refunded.

amount.description: Requested amount in `currency`.

currency.description: Payment currency code.

fee_usd.description: Processing fee for this payment, converted to USD and expressed as a decimal string. `null` until the payment settles.

payment_method.description: Payment method used for this payment.

meta.description: Public metadata stored for this payment.

message.description: Human-readable payment message derived from status.

name.description: Full name of the customer associated with this payment, when captured.

email.description: Customer email address associated with this payment, when captured.

line_items.description: The line items this payment covers.

subscription_id.description: The subscription this payment belongs to, or `null` for a one-time purchase.

invoice.description: The invoice this payment collected. Present only for subscription payments; `null` for one-time purchases.

refunds.description: IDs of any refunds issued for this payment. `null` if no refund has been created.

status_history.description: Chronological list of status changes for this payment.

status.description: Status at this point in time.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the status change, if available.

created_at.description: Creation timestamp.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp.

PaymentListItemResponse.description: Paginated list representation of a payment record.

reference.description: Checkout reference for this payment when available.

id.description: payment ID for retrieval and reconciliation.

status.description: Current payment status for this payment.

is_refundable.description: Whether this payment is currently eligible for refund operations.

amount.description: Requested payment amount in `currency`.

customer_name.description: Customer full name from checkout data. May be empty when unavailable.

customer_email.description: Customer email from checkout data. May be empty when unavailable.

amount_paid.description: Amount received so far for this payment.

amount_remaining.description: Remaining amount expected before full completion.

settlement_amount.description: Settlement-side amount captured for this payment.

fee.description: Reserved fee field in list responses. May be null.

vat.description: Reserved VAT field in list responses. May be null.

currency.description: Payment currency code.

meta.description: Public metadata attached to the payment when available.

transaction_date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the payment was created.

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the payment reached a successful terminal state.

PaymentPagination.description: Pagination details for a payments list.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, or `null` on the last page.

prev_cursor.description: Cursor for the previous page, or `null` on the first page.

has_more.description: Whether more results exist after this page.

limit.description: The page size that was applied.

returned.description: Number of items returned on this page.

total.description: Total number of payments matching the query.

PaymentInvoiceInfo.description: A subscription invoice this payment collected.

subscription_id.description: The subscription the invoice belongs to.

period_start.description: Start of the billing period, UTC.

period_end.description: End of the billing period, UTC.

kind.description: `cycle`: a regular subscription-period invoice. `proration`: an off-cycle mid-cycle change.

CheckoutCustomer.description: The customer attached to the checkout.

id.description: The customer's ID, once resolved. `null` until a customer is matched or created.

name.description: The customer's name. `null` when not provided.

CheckoutRecurring.description: The recurring cadence when the checkout starts a subscription. `null` for a one-time checkout.

interval_count.description: Number of intervals per billing cycle.

WebhookEndpoint.description: A webhook endpoint: a URL Bachs delivers events to, and the events it is subscribed to.

name.description: A label for the endpoint.

url.description: The HTTPS URL events are delivered to.

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint is active and receiving events.

event_types.description: The events this endpoint is subscribed to.

created_at.description: When the endpoint was created, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the endpoint was last updated, in UTC.

CreateWebhookEndpointRequest.description: Parameters for creating a webhook endpoint.

name.description: A label for the endpoint.

url.description: The HTTPS URL Bachs should deliver events to.

event_types.description: The events to subscribe to. At least one is required.

UpdateWebhookEndpointRequest.description: Fields to update on a webhook endpoint. Only the fields you send are changed.

name.description: A new label for the endpoint.

event_types.description: Replace the subscribed events. At least one if provided.

CreateWebhookEndpointResponse.description: The created endpoint, plus its signing secret. The secret is returned only once, on creation. Store it securely.

name.description: A label for the endpoint.

url.description: The HTTPS URL events are delivered to.

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint is active and receiving events.

event_types.description: The events this endpoint is subscribed to.

created_at.description: When the endpoint was created, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the endpoint was last updated, in UTC.

signing_secret.description: The secret used to sign deliveries to this endpoint. Shown only here, on creation. Use it to verify the `X-Bachs-Signature` header.

WebhookEndpointSecretResponse.description: An endpoint with its current signing secret.

name.description: A label for the endpoint.

url.description: The HTTPS URL events are delivered to.

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint is active and receiving events.

event_types.description: The events this endpoint is subscribed to.

created_at.description: When the endpoint was created, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the endpoint was last updated, in UTC.

WebhookMetricsResponse.description: Delivery metrics for an endpoint over a time range.

WebhookEventListItem.description: A summary of a webhook event across all endpoints.

entity_type.description: The kind of resource the event is about.

entity_id.description: The resource the event is about.

created_at.description: When the event occurred, in UTC.

last_attempt_at.description: When delivery was last attempted, in UTC.

WebhookEventsListResponse.description: A paginated list of webhook events.

WebhookEndpointEventListItem.description: A summary of an event's delivery to one endpoint.

entity_id.description: The resource the event is about.

last_attempt_http_status.description: HTTP status your endpoint returned on the last attempt.

last_attempt_at.description: When delivery was last attempted, in UTC.

WebhookEndpointEventsListResponse.description: A paginated list of events delivered to an endpoint.

WebhookEventAttempt.description: A single delivery attempt for an event.

attempt_no.description: The attempt number (1 is the first).

status.description: Outcome of the attempt.

callback_url.description: The URL the attempt was delivered to.

response_snippet.description: A short snippet of your endpoint's response body.

last_error.description: The error, if the attempt failed.

created_at.description: When the attempt started, in UTC.

updated_at.description: When the attempt finished, in UTC.

WebhookEventDetail.description: A webhook event with its full payload and delivery attempts.

entity_type.description: The kind of resource the event is about.

entity_id.description: The resource the event is about.

created_at.description: When the event occurred, in UTC.

payload.description: The full event payload that was (or would be) delivered.

attempts.description: Every delivery attempt for this event.

ResendWebhookEventResponse.description: The result of re-delivering an event.

status.description: Outcome of the resend request.

DeleteWebhookEndpointResponse.description: Confirmation that an endpoint was deleted.

status.description: Always `deleted`.

TrialPeriod.description: The length of the free trial before the first charge, expressed as a count of time units. For example, `{ "interval": "day", "frequency": 14 }` is a 14-day trial.

interval.description: The unit of time the trial is measured in: `day`, `week`, `month`, or `year`.

frequency.description: How many `interval` units the trial lasts. For example, `interval` `day` with `frequency` `14` is a 14-day trial.

BadRequest.description: Bad Request - Validation errors or invalid request format. Check the `details` object for field-specific validation errors. Common causes: missing required fields, invalid data types, values outside allowed ranges, or invalid formats.

errors.message: Amount must be a positive decimal string

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid, missing, or expired API key. Verify your API key is correctly formatted and included in the Authorization header as `Bearer sk_sandbox_...` or `Bearer sk_live_...`. Check that your key hasn't been revoked.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - API key does not have permission for the requested resource. Verify you're using the correct organization's API key and that you're not trying to access another organization's data.

example.detail: API key does not have permission for this operation

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

NotFound.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist. Verify the resource ID is correct and that it belongs to your organization.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

Conflict.description: Conflict - Duplicate request detected (idempotency). This occurs when the same request is made multiple times with the same idempotency key. The original request's response is returned.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

TooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded. Standard tier allows 100 requests per minute per API key. Wait a few seconds before retrying. Check X-RateLimit-Reset header for when the window resets.

example.detail: Rate limit exceeded. Please retry after a few seconds.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window (will be 0)

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying

InternalServerError.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred while processing the request. Retry with exponential backoff. If the issue persists, contact support with your request context.

example.detail: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed per minute

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current window

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit window resets

X-Connected-Account-ID.description: Optional header to target a connected account. Use API key from parent organization.
